commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Explain passing data with props using a new example and justify the result.
Explain read-only props using a new example and justify the result.
Trace and explain parent-to-child data flow using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Passing data with props** → **Read-only props** → **Parent-to-child data flow**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: Passing data with props is best understood as follows: Props are inputs supplied by a parent. They can contain values, objects, functions, or elements and form the child's public data contract.
choices.label: Props are inputs supplied by a parent. They can contain values, objects, functions, or elements and form the child's public data contract.

choices.label: Evaluate alternatives by complexity, clarity, testability, performance, team understanding, and likely change rather than searching for a universally perfect pattern.

choices.feedback: Read-only props is best understood as follows: A component treats props as immutable snapshots for a render. Mutating them creates hidden coupling with the owner.
choices.label: A component treats props as immutable snapshots for a render. Mutating them creates hidden coupling with the owner.

choices.feedback: Parent-to-child data flow is best understood as follows: Data normally flows down through props. Children communicate intent upward by invoking callbacks supplied by parents.
choices.label: Data normally flows down through props. Children communicate intent upward by invoking callbacks supplied by parents.

sections.body: Props are inputs supplied by a parent. They can contain values, objects, functions, or elements and form the child's public data contract.
sections.title: Passing data with props

sections.body: A component treats props as immutable snapshots for a render. Mutating them creates hidden coupling with the owner.
sections.title: Read-only props

sections.body: Data normally flows down through props. Children communicate intent upward by invoking callbacks supplied by parents.
sections.title: Parent-to-child data flow

sections.explanation: The child receives read-only data and communicates intent through a callback.
sections.title: Worked example

summary: Props, Immutability, and One-Way Data Flow connects Passing data with props, Read-only props, Parent-to-child data flow.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Props, Immutability, and One-Way Data Flow

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: Props, Immutability, and One-Way Data Flow is part of the mental model learners need before making reliable implementation decisions. It belongs to the **Components, JSX, and Props** module.
